decisions.rationale: At least two groups are non-unique: "Pig" overlaps with push-your-luck, and the bluffing group uses near-duplicate variants ("Perudo"/"Liar's Dice") that would require broader reworking.
"Record" does not fit the ritual/observance sense of the group; replacing it resolves the mismatch without changing structure.
"Launch" overlaps too naturally with the rocket group; replacing it makes the development group unique.
The sports groups contain general baseball/cricket terms rather than bat-specific terms, so broadening those two labels removes the unfair overlap.
The crime-scene group overlaps with evidence/clues because two items are evidence-related rather than scene-setting.
Refining the first label removes Norway's overlap with the Nordic-cross group.

patches.oldValue: Flags: Red, White and Blue Tricolour
patches.newValue: Flags: Horizontal Red, White and Blue Tricolour

decisions.rationale: The artist-based subgenre groupings are broadly non-exclusive across multiple groups, so fixing fairness would require rebuilding much of the puzzle.
The structural families overlap conceptually in several directions (for example dome/frame/shell), so a fair fix would require substantial regrouping.
These board-game mechanics are cross-cutting by nature, and multiple groups would need redesign to become uniquely sortable.
Several therapies span the listed schools, so the taxonomy itself is too overlapping for a small patch.
The start-based groups need to exclude all-dot/all-dash codes, and "CH" should be replaced with a standard Morse symbol for English use.
The puzzle mixes overlapping transformation classes, near-synonyms, and projection types; making it exclusive would require major restructuring.
Several listed terms are dual-use across crimes and torts; replacing them with more exclusive doctrinal examples makes the group boundaries fairer.
"Poitiers" is historically ambiguous without qualification; replacing it with an unambiguous medieval battle fixes the issue cleanly.
The archetype roles overlap across several characters, especially in the trickster group, so a fair solution would need multiple replacements and likely rethinking of the set.
